title: Meetings & Conventions Magazine — 'Planners Turn to Tech: Virtual meetings post-Sept 11' (PSK Aberdeen CRO quoted, November 2001)
author: M.C. (Meetings & Conventions staff)
methodology: Meetings & Conventions monthly piece on planners turning to web/video tools post-9/11. Quotes PSK as 'vice president and chief research officer for The Aberdeen Group, a Boston-based technology consulting firm.' Misspells PSK as 'Peter Kassner' in the body — preserved verbatim as primary-source artifact but documented as known typographical error.
abstract: M&C reports a 49% week-of-attack increase in PlaceWare usage (Barry James Folsom, president); Folsom forecasts 30-35% more business attributable to 9/11 effects. WebEx (Praful Shah, VP strategic marketing) reports a similar jump; safety as the driver. PSK: implementation of virtual meetings and conferences AT ABERDEEN itself increased 25% in the week after Sept 11. Bari Pollack (Expocentric VP client services) says virtual events serve as interim measure when shows are postponed. Billy Pidgeon (Jupiter Media Metrix) hedges that the increase 'probably will continue' but other analysts caution against overestimating sustainability.
importance_rationale: Documents PSK as 'vice president and chief research officer' at Aberdeen Nov 2001 — title arc data point. The 25%-Aberdeen-internal videoconferencing uptick is a rare first-person observation by PSK on his own firm's behavior.
relevance_rationale: Reinforces PSK CRO title (with VP also listed) in late 2001; documents PlaceWare/WebEx pre-Zoom-era market position; preserves a known body-text spelling error (Kassner vs Kastner) for archival fidelity.
prescience_rationale: PSK's 25%-internal-Aberdeen-adoption observation foreshadows the eventual broad-based corporate videoconferencing transition that took 19 more years (2020 pandemic). PlaceWare was acquired by Microsoft in 2003 and became Live Meeting/Lync/Teams; WebEx was acquired by Cisco 2007. Both pre-Zoom platforms named in this 2001 piece either evolved into or were displaced by Microsoft Teams and Zoom in the 2020s.
